content.problemStatement:
You are given a tree consisting of NNN nodes where the value of node iii is AiA_iAi‚Äč.

You are also given an **odd** integer K (1‚Č§K<N)K \ (1 \leq K \lt N)K (1‚Č§K<N).

You can perform the following operation any (possibly zero) number of times:


Select an integer x (‚ąí109‚Č§x‚Č§109)x \ (-10^9 \leq x \leq 10^9)x (‚ąí109‚Č§x‚Č§109) and two nodes iii and j (1‚Č§i‚Č§j‚Č§N)j \ (1 \leq i \leq j \leq N)j (1‚Č§i‚Č§j‚Č§N), such that dist(i,j)=Kdist(i,j)=Kdist(i,j)=K;
Add xxx to both AiA_iAi‚Äč and AjA_jAj‚Äč.
Find the **minimum** value of ‚ąĎi=1N‚ą£Ai‚ą£\sum_{i=1}^{N} |A_i|‚ąĎi=1N‚Äč‚ą£Ai‚Äč‚ą£ you can get after performing any number of operations.

Note that dist(i,j)dist(i,j)dist(i,j) denotes the number of edges in the shortest path between nodes iii and jjj.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains two positive integers NNN and KKK, the number of nodes and the required distance.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers, A1,A2‚Ä¶ANA_1, A_2 \ldots A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč‚Ä¶AN‚Äč ‚ąí-‚ąí denoting the array AAA.
The following next N‚ąí1N-1N‚ąí1 lines contains two integers uuu and vvv, denoting an edge between nodes uuu and vvv. It is guaranteed that the given edges form a tree.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the minimum sum that can be obtained.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 5‚čÖ1055 \cdot 10^55‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: In one operation, select x=‚ąí2x = -2x=‚ąí2 and (i,j)=(1,2)(i,j) = (1,2)(i,j)=(1,2) where dist(1,2)=1dist(1, 2) = 1dist(1,2)=1.
Thus, we make A1=2‚ąí2=0A_1 = 2-2 = 0A1‚Äč=2‚ąí2=0 and A2=2‚ąí2=0A_2 = 2-2 = 0A2‚Äč=2‚ąí2=0.
We achieve the minimum possible value of ‚ąĎi=1N‚ą£Ai‚ą£=0\sum_{i=1}^{N} |A_i| = 0‚ąĎi=1N‚Äč‚ą£Ai‚Äč‚ą£=0.
Test case 222: Initially, ‚ąĎi=1N‚ą£Ai‚ą£=0+‚ą£‚ąí1‚ą£=1\sum_{i=1}^{N} |A_i| = 0+|-1| = 1‚ąĎi=1N‚Äč‚ą£Ai‚Äč‚ą£=0+‚ą£‚ąí1‚ą£=1.
It can be shown that we cannot make the required value less than 111 in any number of operations.
Test case 333: In one operation, choose x=‚ąí1x = -1x=‚ąí1 and (i,j)=(1,4)(i, j) = (1, 4)(i,j)=(1,4). Thus, A1=1‚ąí1=0A_1 = 1-1 = 0A1‚Äč=1‚ąí1=0 and A4=1‚ąí1=0A_4 = 1-1 = 0A4‚Äč=1‚ąí1=0.
We achieve the minimum possible value of ‚ąĎi=1N‚ą£Ai‚ą£=0+‚ą£‚ąí10‚ą£+‚ą£‚ąí10‚ą£+0=20\sum_{i=1}^{N} |A_i| = 0+|-10|+|-10|+0 = 20‚ąĎi=1N‚Äč‚ą£Ai‚Äč‚ą£=0+‚ą£‚ąí10‚ą£+‚ą£‚ąí10‚ą£+0=20.
